title: Medical emergency (student seriously hurt or ill)
tags: emergency hospital ambulance unconscious seizure breathing injury bleeding overdose intoxicated faint collapse 911 urgent passed out unresponsive not waking won't wake breathe choking fell fall stairs head hurt seriously injured can't move
teaser: On campus → Public Safety 212-854-5555. Off campus → 911. Then RD + Maxient.
steps: Stay with the student. On campus, call Columbia Public Safety 212-854-5555 (24/7) — they arrange the emergency response.
If you are off campus, call 911 first, then call your SRA or RD on duty.
Notify the RD on duty right away.

After Health Services hours, students go to Mt. Sinai St. Luke's (Amsterdam Ave). Call the RD on call BEFORE escorting — a Coordinator may need to go too. ER entrance is on 113th St just east of Amsterdam; check the student into pediatrics to speed response.
File a Maxient incident report afterward. Bring phone chargers + something warm to the hospital.

title: Student feels sick (non-emergency, daytime)
tags: ill unwell fever cold nausea headache health services appointment doctor sick clinic john jay
teaser: Walk them to Health Services (John Jay 3rd floor). Floaters: this is priority #1.
steps: Encourage students to report illness during the day — waiting until evening means an ER visit that takes all night.
A student must be accompanied by an RA/program assistant to Health Services (John Jay Hall, 3rd floor).
Tell the receptionist you are with “the Columbia University Pre-College Program.”
Bring the student's health information with you.
Urgent care is seen without an appointment. For non-urgent needs, call to book: 212-854-2284.
If a student says they're too ill for class, they must go to Health Services. Floater priority list ranks Health Center visits #1.

title: Take a student to the health center or hospital (step-by-step)
tags: health center hospital escort sick injured medical john jay st luke lukes emergency room er take student doctor pharmacy medication health chat health form 24 hour line who escorts isolation urgent care bring walk clinic visit appointment infirmary nurse
teaser: Call the 24-hr health line 212-854-7426 first, grab the health form, post in the Health Chat, escort and stay until seen, then file Maxient.
steps: Emergency? Call Public Safety 212-854-5555 first, then your RD. Otherwise notify your RD or post in the Health Chat that a student needs care.

Decide where: John Jay Health Center for mild issues during business hours (Mon–Thu 9am–5pm, Fri 9am–4pm, John Jay Hall — call to reserve before walking in); St. Luke's ER (113th & Amsterdam) for after-hours, weekends, medium/high, or a clear emergency.
Call 212-854-7426 first (24-hr CU Health line) with the student present — explain who you are, then hand the phone to the student to describe symptoms.
Grab the student's health form and bring it. A student must be accompanied by staff the entire time.
Text the Health Chat the student's name + UNI when you take them, and again when you bring them back (doctor's instructions, any meds prescribed).
Stay until the student is seen. At a hospital over 4 hours? Call the Director on duty to be relieved. Submit a Maxient report after every health/wellness incident.
note: Staff cannot give students medication of any kind. If a doctor prescribes meds, notify the Director on duty and escort the student to the pharmacy. You may give basic emergency aid only if you've done the CPR/first-aid training. Verify before relying: the 24-hr CU Health line (212-854-7426) and the St. Luke's ER/Psychiatric ER numbers (212-523-3335 / 212-523-3347) are not in the RA Handbook — confirm them against the SRA Master Contact Sheet or Health Protocols deck. The Handbook lists 212-854-2284 for Health Services appointments and links the Mount Sinai Morningside ER page (no direct ER phone).

title: Student has an allergic reaction
tags: allergy allergies allergic reaction food allergy peanut nut shellfish bee sting wasp insect medication drug allergy epipen epi-pen anaphylaxis hives swelling throat rash breathing health form reaction
teaser: Severe reaction (trouble breathing/swelling/throat) = medical emergency → Public Safety 212-854-5555 / 911 off campus. Otherwise to Health Services. Know your students' allergies in advance from their health forms.
steps: Before the program: review each student's health form to learn their allergies (food, insect/bee-wasp, medication), what reactions they have, how they treat them, and what response you can provide.

If a reaction looks severe — trouble breathing, throat/face/lip swelling, widespread hives, or the student has an EpiPen for it — treat it as a MEDICAL EMERGENCY: on campus call Public Safety 212-854-5555; off campus call 911, then your SRA/RD.
For a milder reaction, accompany the student to Health Services (John Jay, 3rd floor) — call the 24-hr CU Health line 212-854-7426 first with the student present.
Staff cannot administer medication of any kind. The student manages their own EpiPen/antihistamine; you can summon emergency help and stay with them.
If the allergy is food-related, make sure other students on the floor know the necessary safety precautions.
Notify your RD and file a Maxient report for any health incident.
note: Verify before relying: the 'staff cannot administer medication' rule and the 24-hr CU Health line (212-854-7426) are reused from the health-center/hospital card and are not in the RA Handbook itself — confirm the number against the SRA Master Contact Sheet. The Allergies facts (review health forms, wasp/bee/medication allergies, food-allergy precautions) are from the Handbook 'Allergies' section.

title: Student needs to isolate / has COVID
tags: isolation isolate covid covid-19 sick contagious quarantine meal delivery to-go mask isolation needs request form
teaser: Call up to your RD. Isolated students get daily RA meal delivery; extra needs go through the Isolation Needs Request Form (once/day).
steps: If a student needs to isolate, call up to your Resident Director.
Students isolating on campus get daily meal delivery from an RA. Extra food/beverage needs go to the RD via the Isolation Needs Request Form (no more than once a day).
A student with COVID-19 may double-mask to pick up a to-go meal from the cafeteria if they prefer.
File a Maxient report (health/wellness incident).

title: Student overheated / heat exhaustion / dehydration
tags: heat exhaustion heatstroke heat stroke dehydration dehydrated fainting fainted passed out dizzy overheated hot weather sun water hydration hydrate collapse subway summer outdoor excursion
teaser: Heat illness is serious — get them cool and hydrated; if they faint or it's severe, treat as a medical emergency (Public Safety 212-854-5555 / 911 off campus).
steps: NYC summers are very hot and each summer a student or two faints from heat exhaustion — take it seriously, especially if it happens away from a program setting (e.g., on the subway).
If a student faints, can't stand, or shows severe symptoms, treat it as a medical emergency: on campus call Public Safety 212-854-5555; off campus call 911, then your SRA/RD.
Get them to a shaded or air-conditioned spot, have them sit/lie down, and have them drink water.

Prevent it: promote water-drinking, take frequent breaks in shade/AC during activities, and be strict — if needed, stop the activity and make students (and yourself) hydrate. Avoid strenuous outdoor exercise in the heat; do it early morning or evening.
Notify your RD and file a Maxient report for any health incident.

title: Student is emotionally distressed / says they're depressed
tags: crying upset emotional distress depressed depression anxiety mental health homesick sad overwhelmed wellness counselor panic panic attack anxiety attack hyperventilating freaking out can't breathe shaking breakdown
teaser: Console them, then contact the Wellness Coordinator right away + file Maxient. You can't fix it alone.
steps: Number one rule: you cannot help the student on your own — the most caring thing is to reach out to administrators.
If the student appears to be in immediate danger, call Public Safety 212-854-5555 or 911.
Otherwise: console the student, then contact the Wellness Coordinator (Caroline Polak) right away and submit a Maxient report.
Watch the line between supportive and over-your-head: conversation past 15 min, inconsolable crying, no resolution, or repeated nightly → escalate.
DO express concern and share you'll refer them for help. AVOID promising confidentiality, becoming their only support, or saying “it's not that bad.”

title: Student shows suicide warning signs
tags: suicide suicidal self harm self-harm hurt themselves kill killing myself die dying crisis red flag hopeless wellness emergency danger worth living not worth it don't want to be here end it give up better off dead overdose pills
teaser: Take it seriously. Immediate danger → Public Safety/911. Then RD, Wellness Counselor, or Dean Phong Luu + Maxient.
steps: Take any mention of suicide seriously. Talk about it openly and directly — don't minimize their pain.
If the student appears to be in immediate danger, call Public Safety 212-854-5555 or 911 now.
Do not offer assurances of confidentiality you can't honor, and don't become their main source of support.
Contact the RD, Wellness Counselor, or Assistant Dean Phong Luu for significant concerns — together you access professional help.
Submit a Maxient report. Higher-urgency contexts: past attempts, access to lethal means, family history, isolation, recent major stressors.
note: This is a sensitive topic. If you're personally affected, Columbia's CPS and the 988 Suicide & Crisis Lifeline are available.

title: Suspected abuse or maltreatment of a minor
tags: abuse maltreatment minor child protection pom mandated reporter danger hotline neglect abused hit hits hitting beaten beats beating hurt home parent hurts family violence
teaser: You're a mandated reporter. Immediate danger → 911. Then NY State Hotline + POM form + Public Safety.
steps: All staff are mandated reporters — report reasonable suspicion immediately.
If a child is in immediate danger, call 911 and, as appropriate, Public Safety 212-854-5555 (Morningside).
Notify the Director / Assistant Director of Student Affairs, who consults the Executive Director of Protection of Minors (Rachel Ferrari) on reporting procedures.
Call the NY State Child Abuse & Maltreatment Hotline: 800-342-3720 — and write down the ID number and the representative's name.
As directed, call Public Safety and give them the hotline ID number and representative name.
Submit the Protection of Minors report (Maxient) — include documentation, the hotline ID number, and the names of any Public Safety/NYPD officers who responded.
note: Don't call the POM Exec Director directly — the Handbook routes you through the verified numbers above (911, NY State Hotline 800-342-3720, Public Safety 212-854-5555) plus the Director/Assistant Director, who consult the Protection of Minors office on procedure. A direct POM line is not in the RA Handbook; reach Rachel Ferrari by email (rf2814@columbia.edu) or confirm a phone number against the SRA Master Contact Sheet before relying on it.

title: Disruptive, aggressive, or threatening student
tags: aggressive angry violent threat threaten threatened threatening threatens hurt harm punch hit swung physical scary intimidating bullying yelling fight altercation disruptive hostile outburst confrontation
teaser: Stay calm, intervene early. If there's a safety risk, call Public Safety/NYPD immediately.
steps: Remain calm and professional; read verbal and nonverbal cues.
Intervene as soon as you see/hear it and be consistent. Briefly tell the student the behavior is unacceptable and must be resolved.
If in your judgment they create a safety risk, contact Public Safety or NYPD immediately — use a phone out of sight/hearing, or have someone else call.
Consult the SPS Care Team and admin staff for support. File a Maxient report.
AVOID power struggles, getting defensive, or responding with anger.

title: Gender-based misconduct or harassment report
tags: title ix gender sexual harassment assault misconduct stalking discrimination bias eoaa report touched touched me inappropriate inappropriately rape raped groped molested unwanted forced grabbed nonconsensual non-consensual gbm title9 harass harassed harassing sexually creeped coerced pressured
teaser: You're obligated to report. File the GBM form; serious cases go to Public Safety + SCCS.
steps: All staff are obligated to report any misconduct, harassment, or discrimination they become aware of.
Encourage the student to report; immediately call the Program Office or report to the Director of Student Affairs.
File the Gender-Based Misconduct report form (bit.ly/gbmreport).
The report goes to Public Safety and Student Conduct & Community Standards; the GBM Office contacts each party about resolution options.
Do not attempt to mediate Title IX / bias / harassment incidents yourself.

title: Suspected alcohol, drugs, or an intoxicated student
tags: alcohol drunk drinking beer drugs weed marijuana vape vaping smoking cigarette high intoxicated under the influence substance paraphernalia possession smell hungover wasted hammered trashed plastered smashed blackout blacked out tipsy buzzed stoned faded came back drunk threw up vomiting passed out can't stand
teaser: Safety first, not enforcement. Dangerous intoxication = medical emergency → Public Safety. Don't force a search; document + notify RD + Maxient.
steps: Students possessing or using alcohol or illegal drugs is prohibited (everyone here is a minor). Smoking/vaping — and even possessing smoking or vaping devices — is also banned in the residence halls.

Your first job is safety, not enforcement. Signs of dangerous intoxication are a medical emergency — call Public Safety 212-854-5555 (or 911 off campus) now. Never assume an intoxicated student gave accurate info about what or how much they took.
Do not physically search a student or wrestle items away. Calmly ask them to hand over any alcohol/substances/devices; if they refuse or it escalates, step back and contact your SRA/RD.
Notify the SRA and RD on duty and stay with the student until support arrives.
Document observable facts (what you saw/smelled, time, who was present) and file a Maxient report — stick to behavior, avoid labels.

title: When to call Public Safety / NYPD
tags: public safety nypd crisis call when threshold danger help unsure emergency alerts alert notifications text sign up signup
teaser: Call 212-854-5555 if a situation is nearing crisis. When unsure, err on the side of calling.
steps: Call Public Safety 212-854-5555 when behavior poses imminent danger of: harming self or others; impeding others' lawful activities; threatening health/safety/wellbeing; a health emergency; or online posts suggesting suicidal ideation or intent to harm.

Situations that usually warrant a call: violent/suicidal/homicidal behavior, seizures, labored breathing, physical injury, unconsciousness, hallucinations or intense pain, inability to speak/walk, or dangerous intoxication.
If you're unsure whether a crisis exists, err on the side of caution and call.
Make sure you're signed up for Columbia Emergency Alerts so you get real-time safety notifications.

title: Active shooter on campus
tags: active shooter weapon gun lockdown shelter run hide fight emergency gunman gunmen shooting shoot shots fired armed handgun firearm someone with a gun gunshot gunshots heard shots
teaser: Secure the area: lock/barricade doors, stay quiet, then contact authorities when safe.
steps: If outside, take immediate cover, preferably inside a building.
If inside: secure your area — lock and barricade doors, turn off lights, silence phones, stay out of sight and quiet.
Contact authorities (911 / Public Safety 212-854-5555) when you can do so safely; report location, number of shooters, descriptions, and weapons.
Do not open doors until an official all-clear; police entering will be focused on stopping the threat first.
See the full Active Shooter protocol in the RA Handbook.

title: Student is missing / didn't check in
tags: missing student can't find unreachable left building went for a walk didn't sign in disappeared not back search lost gone curfew no show
teaser: 30 min: RD owns outreach. >1 hr after curfew: Director notifies parents + Public Safety.
steps: Residential staff proactively reach out to any student who hasn't signed in at in-building or in-room curfew.
Missing under 30 minutes: the RA/Senior RA makes a direct phone call to the student and notifies the RD on duty.
Missing over 30 minutes: the Resident Director takes over outreach.
Missing over 1 hour after curfew: contact the Director on duty, who notifies the parents/guardian and contacts Public Safety to help locate the student.
File a Maxient report. (Missing under 60 min is a Medium incident; over 60 min is Major.)

title: Incident severity & who responds
tags: incident severity level minor medium major classification escalation who responds matrix low high tier notetaker chain how serious how bad bad enough big deal when to escalate when to call rd worth reporting serious enough wake the rd judgment call
teaser: Minor / Medium / Major — the bigger it is, the higher it escalates. RA is always on site; RD, Director, then Senior Leadership loop in by severity.
steps: Minor (roommate conflict, curfew, minor policy/health, ongoing depression/anxiety): the RA addresses it, notifies the RD (on campus) or Director (off campus), and files a Maxient report.
Medium (urgent non-emergency medical, hospital visit, escalated conflict, disruptive behavior, serious policy violation, theft, missing under 60 min): RA + RD on site; the Director is informed.

Major (hospitalization, urgent mental health, assault, drugs/alcohol, building/campus emergency, missing over 60 min, student death, Protection of Minors): RA + RD + Director on site; Senior Leadership informed.

For a major incident, the rhythm is: stay with the student, make your calls (Public Safety on campus / 911 off campus, then RD/Director), then become the notetaker once your supervisor arrives, and file a Maxient report.
details: Who responds, by severity Low: RA / Senior RA on site · RD / Coordinator informed Medium: RA + RD / Coordinator on site · Director informed High: RA + RD + Director on site · Senior Leadership informed

title: Calling NYPD / police (theft, robbery, off-campus crime)
tags: nypd police precinct theft robbery robbed robbing stolen crime report shoplifting off campus mugged file report law enforcement assault harassment stole took my phone pickpocket pickpocketed snatched grabbed my bag train subway street wallet purse phone bag
teaser: Off-campus crime → nearest NYPD precinct ASAP. On-campus crime in progress → Public Safety then 911. Already happened → Public Safety escorts you to the precinct.
steps: Off-campus incidents (theft, harassment, robbery): immediately contact or go to the nearest NYPD precinct to file a report — don't delay.
On campus, crime in progress / emergency: call Public Safety 212-854-5555 first, then 911 to request NYPD.
On campus, already occurred: report to Public Safety, who will escort you to the right precinct to file.
You may have to call NYPD yourself or explicitly ask for NYPD involvement.
On an excursion: report to MTA/police before leaving the station, stay for the report, call the Director on duty, and file a Maxient.

title: Student feels unsafe / harassed off campus (subway, street)
tags: unsafe harassed harassment followed scared subway train street stranger creepy uncomfortable separated lost catcalled safe haven scam pickpocket mugged off campus excursion
teaser: Move away, don't engage, use the 'Sorry, I have to go' exit; duck into a Columbia Safe Haven (red-lion decal) store; call your SRA. Off-campus incident → report to MTA/police before leaving, call Director on duty, file Maxient.
steps: If uncomfortable, just move — you don't have to talk to or stay near anyone. If someone is talking to you, say "Sorry. I have to go." and leave. Don't engage strangers; avoid staring at or photographing anyone behaving strangely.

If you feel unsafe or lost: approach a police officer, call your SRA, or step into a store. Near Columbia, look for Columbia Safe Haven stores — identified by a red-lion 'Columbia Safe Haven' decal in the window — that provide a secure place for students.
Getting back to campus: take the 1 train (stops right outside Columbia). Do NOT ride the 2/3 past 96th St (transfer to the 1 at 96th); the A/C to 110/116 are far from campus.

If something happens on a train/bus: report to an MTA employee or police officer before leaving the station — do not leave without making a report. Stay on scene for the report with all witnesses present.
Call the Director on duty immediately — don't wait until returning to campus — and file a Maxient report for every off-campus incident, big or small.

title: Confronting a policy violation (step-by-step)
tags: confront policy violation enforce enforcement rule breaking caught student misbehaving address stop behavior id uni document witnessed de-escalate disruptive
teaser: Stay calm, identify yourself, ask them to stop, get their ID/UNI, explain the violation, say you're documenting it, set expectations, file an IR.
steps: Identify the issue, take a breath, then approach and introduce yourself as a staff member (give your name).
Ask the student to stop the behavior immediately, then ask for their ID card and UNI.
Tell them what you observed, ask for clarification, and explain why it violated a policy.
Let them know you're documenting it and filing an Incident Report, and set expectations for the rest of the night.
File the Incident Report (Maxient). Gather the CUIDs of all students involved (regardless of participation), photos, and the names of responding staff/officers; use your phone to note times.

Do: stay calm, be firm and clear, take notes, de-escalate, get help if needed. Don't: match aggression, involve too many people, promise an outcome, reframe it as “okay,” or ever physically touch a student.

title: Government checkpoint / ICE, media requests, or pushy parents
tags: ice immigration government checkpoint agent agents officer officers deport deportation detained showed up outside knocking warrant uscis cbp on campus law enforcement media press reporter photograph film interview parent inquiry external risk minor amanda diaz
teaser: ICE/checkpoint: on campus → Public Safety + Director; off campus → say the script + call Amanda Díaz. Media → columbianews@. Parents → After-Hours line / Student Services.
steps: Government checkpoint or ICE — on campus: contact Public Safety and the Director on Duty.

Off campus: state — “I am a staff member from Columbia University's Pre-College Program. You are currently speaking with a minor in an academic program.” Then call Amanda Díaz and follow up with the pro-staff on call.
Media requests: direct them to columbianews@columbia.edu. Students have the right not to be photographed, filmed, or interviewed — advance written permission from each student is required.
Parent interactions with RAs should be limited — direct parent inquiries to the After-Hours parent line or the Student Services Office.

title: When (and when not) to call a parent
tags: parent parents notify notification guardian call home tell parents communication when not attendance behavior sexual contact abuse confidential family
teaser: Usually notify parents on attendance/health/behavior — but the student's safety at home comes first; loop in admin before notifying in sensitive cases.
steps: In most cases staff communicate attendance, health, and behavior issues to a parent/guardian — but always weigh the student's safety at home and the right timing and method.

Some situations where safety takes precedence over notifying parents: consensual student–student sexual contact (if telling home could endanger the student), and Protection of Minors (a child reporting abuse at home).
For abuse-at-home / POM: contact the Director of Student Affairs (Programs for Minors) before deciding whether to call parents — there may be a continued threat at home.
Parental-notification calls aren't made alone — when unsure, consult your RD/Director.

title: Student is late to curfew
tags: curfew late missing check-in tracker incident 30 minutes night room building
teaser: Under 30 min late → curfew tracker form. Over 30 min → Incident Report + RD follow-up.
steps: Curfew checks must be face-to-face — never by text, group chat, or another RA without supervisor approval.
If a student is under 30 minutes late → log it in the curfew tracker form.
If over 30 minutes late → complete an Incident Report (Maxient) and follow up with your building's RD.
Stay in the hall during and between curfew checks.

title: Curfew times & dining hours
tags: curfew time hours schedule in-building in-room dining ferris booth breakfast lunch dinner food meal
teaser: In-building: 9pm Sun–Thu / 10pm Fri–Sat. In-room: 11pm / 12am. Curfew ends 6am.
details: Curfew In-building (RAs must be in-building for checks): Sun–Thu 9:00 PM · Fri–Sat 10:00 PM In-room (RAs off after checks unless on duty): Sun–Thu 11:00 PM · Fri–Sat 12:00 AM Curfew ends 6:00 AM daily. Ferris Booth dining (Lerner Center) Meals are served at the Ferris Booth Dining Hall in the Lerner Student Center (CUID access). Dining hours can change each session — check the live Columbia Dining page for current breakfast/lunch/dinner times before sending students.
note: Dining hours vary by session and aren't fixed in the Handbook — verify current Ferris Booth hours on the live Columbia Dining page before relying on them.

title: What students can & can't do during in-room curfew
tags: in-room curfew in room curfew visiting rooms visit each other's rooms another student's room hang out in a room someone else's room door propped open door open roommate permission double roommate agreement wrong floor own floor other floor different floor not their floor on the wrong floor student i don't recognize unfamiliar student write-up writeup restricted floors residential floor lounge common area shower take a shower bathroom restroom toilet step out to the bathroom water water fountain get water escort downstairs john jay wallach hallway bathrooms after curfew where are they within the building checked in safe in the building between in-room and in-building can a student go to another floor at night allowed in someone else's room can students visit each other
teaser: After in-room curfew, students must be in their OWN room. Before in-room they can visit another student's room only with everyone's permission and the door propped open. Students may only be on their own residential floor — wrong floor = a write-up.
steps: Floors are restricted: students may only be on their own residential floor. Hanging out happens in lounges / common areas (which is why those get staffed). A student actively on the wrong floor — or one you don't recognize — is a write-up: have the conversation and document it.

Visiting rooms: students can be in each other's rooms only BEFORE in-room curfew, and only with full permission from everyone who lives there (in a double, both roommates must agree — they sort this out in the roommate agreement at opening).
If students hang out in someone else's room, the door must be propped open. If it can't be propped open, they can't stay in that room.

At in-room curfew, everyone must be back in their own assigned room. After in-room, students don't need to tell you where they are within the building — once they're checked in, you know they're safe inside.

Bathroom: in John Jay / Wallach (hallway bathrooms), a student stepping out to use the facilities is fine — never block a student from the bathroom, and don't write them up for it. Reinforce that showers should happen BEFORE in-room curfew.
Water in John Jay: there's no in-room/floor fountain, so escort the student downstairs to the fountain and back up — don't let minors go down alone.
note: New scenario; curfew TIMES live in the “Curfew times & dining hours” card. “Door must be propped open” is a mid-session correction from Quinn; high-confidence transcript quotes. No phone numbers added.

title: Orah app — curfew roll checks, passes & student locations
tags: orah orah app roll call roll check roll checks check in students at curfew curfew check-in app what app do we use roll call app staff version parent version student version present absent excused absence late mark them check my students check my group my assigned students whole floor not checked in unchecked missing from roll call not showing up student locations out of bounds pass out-of-bounds pass off-campus pass confirm a pass verify a pass same name two students same name identify by room number app.orah.com web browser RDs say my kids aren't checked in scheduled pass parent out of bounds form night off cover check-ins
teaser: RAs use the STAFF version of the Orah app to run curfew roll checks (present / absent / excused / late), confirm out-of-bounds passes, and verify student locations. RDs see live who's still unchecked — watch the chat.
steps: Use the STAFF version of Orah (there are separate parent, student, and staff versions in the app store — the staff one shows ALL students). You can also use it in a browser at app.orah.com.

At each curfew the RD starts a roll call ~10–15 min early (e.g. ~8:45–8:50 for a 9pm in-building check). You'll get a notification + a note in the chat — open the roll, find your building, then your floor.
You're responsible for checking in only YOUR own assigned group (usually ~4–7) — not the whole floor. Exception: if a floormate is on their night off, you may cover their check-ins.

Lay eyes on each of your students and mark them present / absent / excused absence / on an approved program or pass. Identify students by ROOM number, not just name — students often share the exact same first/last name, and some go by a different name than their legal name in the app.

RDs and SRAs see live how many are still unchecked; the roll isn't closed until everyone is accounted for. If an RD says they're still missing people, go back and click in whoever's outstanding (don't be surprised by a call that none of your kids are checked in).

A student missing from the roll? Check their profile for an active or scheduled out-of-bounds pass first — students on an approved pass may not appear (a banner shows when a pass is active). Office staff schedule passes only after a parent submits the out-of-bounds form and they confirm by phone; students cannot add their own passes. If there's no pass, it may be a troubleshooting issue — flag it to your SRA / a returner.
Running late from a program and can't check in on time? Post it in the chat and ask a floormate to check your students (see the late-from-program / shift-cover guidance).
note: App name is “Orah” (auto-transcripts garble it as oral/aura/ora; the lockout card already uses “Orah” — standardized on that). Curfew TIMES are in the “Curfew times & dining hours” card; late thresholds are in the “Student is late to curfew” card. Confirm exact app behavior with Jeannette, who runs the hands-on Orah practice session, before relying on edge cases.

title: You're on overnight duty / on call
tags: duty on call overnight rounds report ringer night shift 8:50 maxient building rounds
teaser: Check in with RD, do rounds at 15/45/75 min after curfew, ringer ON, duty report at 8:50am.
steps: Duty runs from in-room curfew until the morning lounge shift begins (about twice a week).
Check in with the RD on duty for any special instructions.
Complete floor/building rounds at 15, 45, and 75 minutes after curfew; tell your SRA each time you finish a round.
Keep your ringer ON and off Do-Not-Disturb all night so you can answer calls.
Send an updated report if anything happens overnight. Submit your duty report at 8:50 AM; all Maxient reports in before 9:00 AM.
Never leave the building while on duty — not even for a food delivery. Pick up food before in-building curfew. Leaving = formal HR write-up.

title: Getting a night off / time off (and July 3–4)
tags: night off day off time off vacation request coverage substitute curfew shifts holiday july 3 july 4 fourth of july independence day fireworks work weekend rest break burnout
teaser: You get at least one night off per week — request it from your SRA/RD and arrange your own coverage. All staff MUST work July 3 and July 4 — no day off either day.
steps: You're given at least one night off per week during the Practicum to step back and focus on yourself; another RA covers your curfew checks.
To take it, send a request to your SRA or RD. Specific days are not guaranteed — a night off only happens if a substitute RA is available to cover your area.
After your request, make sure you have no assigned shifts that day — it is YOUR responsibility to find coverage (see the 'Swap or cover a shift' card).
July 3 and July 4: all staff are required to work both days — the program cannot allocate either as a day off for anyone.
Struggling or burned out? You can talk to your SRA, RD, Jeannette, or Phong — they'd rather you ask for support than push through in silence.

title: Lobby table & floater shifts — what to do
tags: lobby table shift floater shift station post desk daytime greeting entry exit building access elevator phone napping pajamas student services office contact chain rounds lobby duty what do
teaser: Lobby: staff your station, enforce in-building curfew, help with lockouts/access, phone on, no napping/pajamas. Floater: wait in Student Services Office for calls (escorts, food pickup, errands).
steps: Lobby table: staff your assigned station — greet entry/exit, enforce in-building curfew, help with building access and lockouts, escort Facilities, keep lounges tidy, manage elevator loads, update bulletin boards, and answer policy questions.
Lobby rules: keep your phone on (lockout / health-form calls), no napping, video games, or leaving the building except on official breaks, and dress appropriately (no pajamas).
Lobby contact chain for issues: Mon–Fri 9–5 → Building RD → SRD → Jeannette Sanchez. After 5 PM / weekends → RD On Duty.

Floater shift (Morning 9am–1pm / Afternoon 1–5pm): when you're not on a task, wait in the Student Services Office with your phone on. Tasks include student escorts, food pickup, errands, and program assistance.

title: RA–student boundaries (money, personal questions, social media, “keep it secret”)
tags: boundaries boundary money lend cash gift personal questions dating crush social media instagram follow friend hang out contact number professional near peer friendly not friends confidential secret don't tell mandated reporter relationship favoritism
teaser: “Friendly, but not friends.” Never lend money or give gifts; don't share your dating life or socialize/DM students; redirect crushes kindly but firmly; you can't promise secrecy.
steps: Your stance is “friendly, but not friends.” You're a guide, resource, role model, and first-responder — not their peer, therapist, parent, or social-media buddy. There's a real power imbalance, so keep it professional.

Money: never lend money or pay for a student's meals/items, and don't give cash gifts. Instead help them solve it (grab their wallet, a friend spots them) or refer to campus resources / contact admins about meal-plan issues.

Personal questions / a crush: don't answer questions about your dating life — redirect. No exchanging personal contact info, socializing outside the program, adding students on personal social media, or any romantic/sexual relationship. Address it kindly but firmly and document concerning interactions.

Try: “I appreciate that you feel comfortable with me, but I'm here as your RA and mentor — I need to keep this professional. I won't be exchanging personal contact info or hanging out outside the program.”

“Don't tell anyone” / confidentiality: you're a mandated reporter — never promise secrecy you can't keep; say so upfront. You must report suspected abuse/neglect, self-harm or suicidal ideation, illegal drug use or distribution, sexual assault/harassment, and any criminal activity. Student safety always beats confidentiality.
When unsure, lean on program policy, avoid making exceptions, and involve your supervisor. File a Maxient report for anything concerning.

title: Roommate / student conflict (mediation)
tags: roommate conflict mediation argument dispute disagreement fight tension agreement mediate roommates fighting argue arguing hate don't get along not getting along beef drama two students between students personality clash
teaser: Address within 24h. Prep, update supervisor, mediate, write an IR, follow up. Don't mediate physical/bias/Title IX.
steps: Prioritize mediations and address conflict within 24 hours.
Prep: gather info from those involved, narrow to immediate parties, update your supervisor, reserve a space (e.g. floor lounge), set a time limit (≤1 hr).
During: open with intro + ground rules, give equal time, let residents brainstorm solutions, review/edit the Roommate Agreement, plan follow-up.
After: update your supervisor on the outcome, write an Incident Report, and check in on the students within 24–48 hours.
Do NOT mediate physical altercations, bias/harassment, or Title IX incidents — escalate those.

title: Student is skipping or refusing to go to class
tags: class skipping skip absent attendance refuse won't go cutting missing lecture academic truant won't get up oversleeping seminar
teaser: Attendance is required unless ill. “Too sick for class” = must go to Health Services. Parents are notified of absences; log it + loop in your supervisor.
steps: Students must attend class unless they're ill. Parents/legal guardians are notified of absences, and multiple missed sessions can cost a student their Certificate of Completion or Letter of Evaluation — even if excused.
If a student says they're too sick for class, they must go to Health Services (accompanied by an RA/PA) — they can't simply stay in their room.
If it's not illness (oversleeping, avoidance, low motivation), encourage and help problem-solve; persistent refusal or withdrawal can signal a deeper concern.
Notify your SRA/RD, and file an Incident Report for repeated absences or a student of concern so the CARE team can follow up.

title: International student homesick / struggling to adjust
tags: international homesick missing home culture shock language slang english accent withdrawn left out belonging adjust food family academic participation plagiarism cheating subway safe nyc tipping tax banking visa customs welcome inclusion racism office hours
teaser: Validate, normalize the adjustment curve, connect them to community + Wellness, and check in proactively.
steps: Watch for: loss of appetite, skipping social events or class, missing home/food/family, withdrawing, or talk of going home.
Normalize the adjustment curve (honeymoon → frustration → adjustment → adaptation) and validate without minimizing. They're only here a short time, so check in proactively and repeatedly.
Loop in your SRA and the Wellness Coordinator for ongoing support; if it deepens into distress, console and file a Maxient + contact Wellness right away.
Help them find belonging — community groups, clubs, study partners, buddies on the floor.
details: Common challenges & how to help Culture shock / homesickness: normalize their feelings, share resources, keep checking in. Academic adjustment: US classes expect participation/discussion, and what counts as “plagiarism/cheating” differs by country. Encourage office hours and asking instructors what's allowed. Communication: go easy on slang/idioms (sports idioms especially), check for understanding, don't correct their English, and watch for inside jokes in groups. Living in NYC: help with subway/MetroCard, tipping & sales tax, and where to shop. If they're anxious about subway/city safety, listen without judgement, give context vs. sensational news, and share street-smart tips. Visa / travel / belonging: be welcoming, celebrate differences without “othering,” redirect hurtful jokes, and thank students when they're vulnerable enough to share.

title: Run a floor meeting
tags: floor meeting agenda hall meeting residents announcements community building weekly check-in
teaser: Use the Floor Meeting agenda to structure it; pair with the programming requirements.
steps: Use the Floor Meeting agenda to structure your meeting so you cover everything.
Hit announcements, curfew/policy reminders, upcoming programs, and a quick community-building moment.
Check the Programming Parameters & Requirements for what's expected of you each week.

title: Health & safety room inspections
tags: health safety inspection room check walkthrough cleanliness contraband fridge notice rooms
teaser: Routine room walkthroughs — follow the inspection protocol, document, and report concerns.
steps: Health & safety inspections are routine room walkthroughs to keep students safe and rooms livable.
Follow the Health and Safety Inspections Protocol for what to check and how to give notice.
Document anything of concern, notify your SRA/RD, and file a Maxient report for any policy violation or student of concern.

title: Move-out — check a student out of their room
tags: move-out moveout move out check-out checkout linens linen room inspection damage cleanliness roster end of session leaving departure last day pack dismissed session a session b
teaser: Collect linens, inspect the room for cleanliness/damage, then check the student out on the move-out roster. Move-out deadline is 9pm Friday.
steps: Students may begin checking out after their last class of the day (most finish by 3:00pm; a smaller group at 5:00pm) and must be packed and out of the dorm by 9:00 pm on the Friday of move-out (July 18 for Session A, Aug 8 for Session B).
For each of your students: collect their linens.
Inspect the room for cleanliness and any damages.
Officially check the student out on the move-out roster.
You're dismissed from your move-out shift once your last student has moved out.

title: Gym access & hours (Dodge Fitness)
tags: gym fitness dodge workout exercise pool athletics access hours recreation dodge fitness center swipe waiver gym waiver get into the gym can't get in cuid won't work before going to the gym
teaser: All staff and students can use Dodge Fitness Center — but you must submit the gym waiver first, then tap your CUID to get in.
steps: Submit the gym waiver first. Without it you cannot access Dodge Fitness — even with your CUID.
All staff and students have access to the Dodge Fitness Center once the waiver is in.
Get in by tapping your CUID at the entrance.
The gym opens before morning training, so early workouts are possible.
Check current location and hours on the Canvas page / Dodge Fitness site.
note: The gym waiver is required before first use — find the form via the Canvas gym page or ask your SRA.

title: Flex dining account
tags: flex dining dollars money balance id pay food meal declining balance merchants account add funds
teaser: Flex is a dollar-for-dollar balance on your CUID — use it at all 10 dining halls + some merchants.
steps: Flex is a declining-balance account Columbia students/faculty/staff can add funds to anytime.
Pay with your CUID at all 10 dining locations and a number of on/off-campus merchants.
See the full list of Flex locations on the Canvas page.

title: Lockout — let a student into their room
tags: lockout locked out room key master key access card door can't get in orah
teaser: Use the Master Key (held by SRA on duty). Verify roster + ID first. After hours → Housing Coordinator.
steps: The hall Master Key is held by the SRA on duty.
Before letting anyone in, verify on the building roster or Orah that they're assigned to that room, and confirm their identity.
Confirm the student has their ID in the room, or arrange an escort to Kent Hall (business hours) if the ID is lost.
Return the Master Key to the storing staff member immediately after.
After hours / weekends: contact the Housing Coordinator on call about a temporary access card.

title: Lost ID / CUID card
tags: lost id card cuid replace access kent furnald re-encode swipe
teaser: New card at ID Office (Kent Hall), then re-encode housing access at Furnald Hall.
steps: Get a replacement at the ID Office in Kent Hall.
After getting the new card, go to Furnald Hall to have it re-encoded with your housing access.
Never let anyone (interns, staff, or students) borrow your ID card.
Your CUID grants access to campus, residence halls, Ferris Booth, Uris, Lerner, and Dodge Fitness.

title: Dorm maintenance issue
tags: maintenance broken ac air conditioning leak flooding repair facilities work order housing dorm room hot water door battery master key hospitality desk service request guided access toilet clogged overflowing plumbing sink drain shower light lightbulb bulb outlet electrical heat heating window blinds pest bug
teaser: Routine → maintenance request (pick “undergraduate housing”) + add the minor/guided-access note. After-hours one-offs (no hot water, dead door battery) → Hospitality Desk 212-854-2779. Emergency → security desk + RD.
steps: Routine problems (AC, cleaning, condition): submit a maintenance request at housing.columbia.edu → “Submit a Service Request” (sign in with UNI → “Columbia Undergraduate Housing Maintenance”), or contact the Housing Coordinator.

Important — add this to the end of the request: “MINOR IN ROOM; PLEASE HAVE RA IN LOBBY PROVIDE GUIDED ACCESS INTO THE SPACE BETWEEN 7AM–MIDNIGHT” and include an RA contact number for guided access. (RAs handle non-emergency requests, lockouts, and escorting Facilities.)
One-off / after-hours issues (dead door battery, no hot water, Master Key problems): call the 24-hour Hospitality Desk 212-854-2779.
Emergency (untraceable gas smell, electrical problem, flooding): go straight to the security desk, have the Public Safety guard call Facilities/emergency personnel, then notify your RD.

Gas smell: check the lounge stove for a turned knob — if that's it, shut it off and open windows (no emergency personnel needed). If you can't find the source, leave immediately, then contact a Public Safety guard and the RD.

title: P-card purchases & receipts
tags: pcard p-card receipt money budget purchase finance reimburse spending excursion meals card
teaser: Submit receipts via the receipt form ASAP + hand to Finance Coordinator same/next day. Lost → missing receipt form.
steps: Every p-card purchase must be followed by immediate receipt submission. Try to get one event onto one receipt.
Photograph the receipt the moment you get it, submit via the receipt form, and hand the physical receipt to the Finance Coordinator (Dhriti Gupta) the same or next business day.
Lost/missing receipt → complete the missing receipt form and turn it in ASAP.
P-cards on excursions are for actual meals only — not snacks or drinks (water for heat/safety is the exception).
If a p-card switches hands, notify the Finance Coordinator immediately. Not submitting receipts or losing a p-card → disciplinary meeting.

title: P-card check-out & meal limits (excursions)
tags: pcard p-card checkout check out sign out meal limit cap budget allowance spend cost how much per meal breakfast lunch dinner receipt itemized gratuity tip split tab return times borrower responsible
teaser: Meal caps: $25 breakfast / $35 lunch / $50 dinner. Itemized receipts, ≤18% tip, last 4 digits shown. You return what you sign out.
steps: Tickets/food on the p-card are for you and other RAs on the trip only — students pay for their own.
Meal caps: Breakfast $25 · Lunch $35 · Dinner $50. For dinner, ask for split tabs (call ahead to confirm the restaurant can).
Receipts must be itemized, tip ≤18%, and show the card's last 4 digits — photograph every receipt.
Sign-out times: Mon–Fri 9:30AM–4:00PM. Used before 5PM (M–F) → return same day; after 5PM (M–Th) → return by 11AM next day; weekend cards (Fri–Mon) → out before 4PM Fri, back by 10AM Mon.
The primary borrower is responsible for ALL transactions until the card is returned, and the RA who signs it out must return it. Tip: groupchat everyone who borrows it to collect receipts.

title: Submit your hours / timesheet
tags: timesheet hours pay vndly tlam log payroll submit sunday clock nextsource onboarding paycheck
teaser: Submit hours in VNDLY by Sunday midnight, then note them in the RA logging sheet.
steps: Submit your hours in VNDLY by Sunday midnight. If you're on call Sunday, go ahead and submit for Monday.
Columbia students also submit in TLAM.
Note what you did and your total hours in the RA logging sheet as a backup.
Reflect your unpaid 30-min break on your timesheet for any formal shift.
Onboarding or pay-vendor questions go to Nextsource (email preferred: onboarding@nextsource.com · 212-851-1553).

title: How many hours can I work / clock for payroll?
tags: hours week clock payroll work maximum max limit cap 20 hours twenty overtime part time full time summer recess paid wage how many hours allowed weekly multiple positions break six consecutive
teaser: Up to 20 hrs/week across all jobs while classes are in session; unlimited during recess (incl. summer) with manager approval. F/J-visa students enrolled for summer = part-time only.
steps: You may work on-campus up to 20 hours per week across all positions during the academic year (fall/spring) when classes are in session.
During recess periods — winter break or summer vacation — you can work unlimited hours with advance approval from your manager(s). So the summer IBC program is not capped at 20.

F-1/J-1 visa exception: if your program requires you to be enrolled during the summer term, it's not a vacation period and you're restricted to part-time hours. You're responsible for any limits tied to your visa/enrollment.
Take a 30-minute unpaid break after 6 consecutive hours (NY law) — not at the start or end of the shift — and log it on your timesheet.
Hold more than one campus job? You'll have separate timesheets — report each department's hours on its own timesheet.
Deadlines differ by system: VNDLY by Sunday midnight; Columbia PAC/TLAM is every other Friday by 3 PM. Late = delayed pay. Payroll/HR questions → submit an SPS HR ticket.

title: Swap or cover a shift
tags: shift swap cover coverage trade schedule can't make change duty staffing concern colleague rebekah saily program coordinator email cc calendar approve curfew lobby floater who approves deadline how late ahead days before notice last minute day of emergency final approved still mine still yours like for like same type
teaser: Email your RD with which shifts + who's taking them, CC your SRA + partner. Request swaps 2–3 days ahead. The shift stays yours until you get the email saying it's approved. Swap like-for-like (lobby for lobby, floater for floater).
steps: All swaps go to your RD by email: include which shifts you're swapping and who's taking each, and CC your SRA, your swap partner, and their SRA/RD if different.
Send swaps 2–3 days ahead so everyone's aware — don't send a day-of or hours-before request unless it's a genuine emergency.
Who approves which shift: Lobby, Floater, and Duty/On-Call → your RD; Curfew checks → your SRA; Major Programming → Program Coordinators; Columbia Travels → RD Rebekah; RA Workshops → RD Saily.
The shift is still yours until you get the email confirming the swap is approved — coverage isn't real until it's approved, so don't assume you're off.
Swap like-for-like: lobby for lobby, floater for floater. Don't trade a lobby shift for a floater shift.
You can't cover someone's curfew/duty shift unless it's your night off. Arrange your own coverage — the swap isn't final until your RD approves.

Keep every shift — original and swapped — on your calendar and share it with your SRA (lobby, floater, programming, workshops, Columbia Travels, duty, curfew, residential programming) so the team knows where you are and your hours stay accurate.
Staffing concern or an issue with a colleague? Use the Staff Concerns Report Form.

title: Plan an excursion or in-building program
tags: excursion trip program proposal budget activity event plan ratio chaperone nyc subway metrocard parameters carman wallach john jay how much money per program in-building program cost spend per student admission ticket how far in advance deadline submit business days approval sra approve rd approve approved pending denied receipts evaluation supplies p-card $150 $125 $500 $75 three miles
teaser: 1 RA : 5 residents. Submit your building's Program Proposal Form + budget for RD approval first; submit at least 3 business days ahead (reviewers have up to 2 business days). Budget figures below are transcript-only — verify with programming/finance.
steps: Maintain a 1 RA to 5 residents ratio on every trip.
Submit your building's Program Proposal Form (Carman / Wallach + Lower John Jay / Upper John Jay) with a budget, and get RD approval before spending. Uphold the approved budget.

Timing (verify): submit the proposal no later than 3 business days before the program; reviewers have up to 2 business days to respond, so submit early. Don't buy anything until you get the email decision (approved / pending changes / denied).

Approval tiers (transcript-only — confirm): your SRA can approve an excursion if it's under ~$75 per staff member, within ~3 miles of campus, and fewer than 10 students. Anything over that — higher cost, farther, very crowded, or 10+ students — goes to your RD.

Budget caps (TRANSCRIPT-ONLY — verify with finance): up to ~$150 per in-building program (per program, not per staff); up to ~$125 per staff member on an excursion; ~$500 total per staff member for the session. Track spend through your SRA.
Students pay their own ticket/admission costs — keep their cost to roughly $100–$125 max, and mix in free programs so anyone can attend.
After the program: keep attendance records, complete the evaluation form, submit receipts digitally, and remove physical flyers once the event has passed.
For supplies, check the building RA office first (paint, canvases, leftover supplies); in-person with the P-card is often faster than ordering online.
Check the Programming Parameters & Requirements and the Approved Excursions List when planning.
Emphasize in-building programming for residents who can't afford/attend excursions.
Brief students on safety before leaving (charged phone, buddy system, MetroCard in advance, meet-up spot, head counts).

Off-campus incident: assess, call 911 if an emergency, report to MTA/police before leaving the station, stay for the report, call the Director on duty immediately, and file a Maxient for any off-campus incident.
note: Budget figures (~$150/in-building program, ~$125/staff per excursion, ~$500/staff per session, ~$75 SRA threshold, ~3 miles, <10 students) and the 3-business-day proposal window come from the 06-09 PM programming training transcript only — NOT the handbook. Confirm with programming/finance before relying on exact amounts; defer to the Programming Parameters doc if it differs.

title: What programs can't be approved
tags: program denied deny rejected not approved won't be approved can't approve banned not allowed prohibited off limits r rated rated r movie film waiver sign a waiver legal contract trampoline park boat ride cliff jumping risky activity out of state new jersey nj state lines mall across state lines five boroughs nyc ratio 5 to 1 five to one staffing curfew after curfew late back returns late dinner right after class classes end floor meeting sunday too late notice business days short notice citi bike citibike city bike bikes p-card personal account what gets rejected why was my program denied is this program ok can i take students
teaser: Some programs are auto-denied: R-rated movies, anything needing a student waiver or a staff-signed contract, out-of-state trips, anything breaking the 5:1 ratio, or returning more than 30 min after curfew.
steps: No R-rated movies — you're supervising minors.

No activities that require a waiver or contract. If students would have to sign a waiver, or staff would have to sign a contract on students' behalf (e.g. cliff jumping, many trampoline parks / boat rides), it needs legal sign-off — so it's generally a no.
No out-of-state trips. Minors aren't taken across state lines — no New Jersey / out-of-state malls. Stay within NYC's five boroughs.
No program that breaks the 5:1 ratio (1 staff per 5 students).
No program that exceeds in-building curfew by more than 30 minutes. (Exception: certain late sports games / major programming, arranged separately.)
No program starting right when classes end — students need time to eat dinner at the dining hall (already paid for), so don't book dinner trips at class-out.
No program that crowds mandatory Sunday floor meetings — students must be back on campus at least 45 minutes before their floor meeting.
Submit with enough lead time — programs need review before they happen (see 'Plan an excursion or in-building program' for the proposal window). Last-minute proposals can't be approved in time.
Citi Bikes: generally avoided. Renting requires putting the P-card on a personal account, which finance policy doesn't allow. Raise specific cases with your RD.
Not sure if your idea qualifies? Run it past your SRA / RD before you plan around it.
note: The 'denied programs' flip side of the 'Plan an excursion' card. From the 06-09 PM programming training transcript (R-rated, waiver/contract, out-of-state, 5:1 ratio, >30-min curfew, dinner timing, 45-min-before-floor-meeting, Citi Bike / P-card). Lead-time step defers to the proposal window in the planning card rather than asserting a separate number. Transcript-only — confirm against the Programming Parameters doc.

title: Metrocards for excursions
tags: metrocard subway transit train fare travel excursion round trip mta finance pick up commute
teaser: Round-trip Metrocards for program travel come from the Finance Coordinator — one RA can grab them for the whole team.
steps: The program provides round-trip Metrocards to staff traveling for excursions or program activities.
Pick them up from the Finance Coordinator (Dhriti Gupta).
If several staff are on one excursion, one person can pick up Metrocards for the whole team.

title: Reserve a space or order catering
tags: room reservation reserve space venue book booking classroom lounge earl hall catering food event uem dodge room auditorium marie rigaud
teaser: Book campus venues through UEM (Earl Hall for SPS); order catering through UEM. POC: Marie Rigaud.
steps: Campus venues (classrooms, lawns, auditoriums, conference rooms): book through University Events Management (UEM) — set up a UEM account first.
Earl Hall has SPS-priority spaces (Dodge Room 14–74, Auditorium 36–230); request via Instructional Support, and you can add Scholastics catering.
Registrar classrooms can't be booked until after the Summer A course-change date (June 12).
Catering: order through UEM (account required). You can buy food on a p-card, but not a catering order. Venues/catering are paid by chartstring.
Point of contact: Marie Rigaud · mr4389@columbia.edu.

title: Facilities — mail, packages, storage, event setup
tags: facilities mail package storage pest control furniture custodial security event setup zendesk sps moving boxes electrical power tablecloth
teaser: SPS Facilities (Zendesk) handles mail/packages/storage/pests; Campus Facilities handles event furniture/custodial/power.
steps: SPS Facilities (primary) handles mail & packages, office maintenance, storage keys, pest control, moving heavy items, and seating. Submit a Zendesk ticket — no payment needed.
Campus Facilities (secondary) helps with event furniture/tablecloth rental, custodial, security, outdoor power/internet, and special-event review. Paid by chartstring.
For student dorm-room issues (AC, leaks), use the Housing Coordinator + maintenance request instead — see the “Dorm maintenance issue” card.

title: Student wants to leave campus / stay overnight
tags: off campus leave overnight pass sign out parent guardian boundaries trip home weekend away
teaser: A parent/guardian must submit the Off-Campus Request Form 72h in advance — students can't self-submit.
steps: A parent or legal guardian must submit the Off-Campus Request Form for each instance, with date/time of departure, destination, and return.
Submit at least 72 hours in advance. The form is only open Mon 9am–Thu 5pm (closed Fri–Sun). Students may NOT submit it themselves.
All requests are verified by direct phone call with the parent/guardian before the student leaves.
Limits: max 2 nights off-campus, one consecutive overnight per session, two out-of-boundary requests per session; students must return before curfew.
Out-of-boundary trips (except medical/lessons) must be supervised by an adult family member 25+. Tourism/exploring with other students is not approved.

title: Student wants a guest or visitor in the building
tags: guest visitor friend parent family girlfriend boyfriend outside person sign in lobby commuter another floor room not allowed someone over
teaser: No visitors in the residence halls — including parents, family, classmates, and other summer programs. Only move-in/move-out days.
steps: Residential students may not have visitors in the residence halls. This includes parents/legal guardians, family members, classmates, and students from other on-campus summer programs.
The only exception: parents/guardians and family may help a student move in or out on the first and last days of the program.
Students may only enter the building, floor, and room they're assigned to — not a friend's floor or room.
Commuter and online students are not permitted in the residence halls at all; commuter students must leave campus daily by 6:30–7:00 PM.
If someone won't leave or you're unsure, contact your SRA/RD and the front-desk Public Safety guard.

title: Which report form do I file?
tags: report form incident maxient which form student of concern gbm pom protection minors conduct violation
teaser: Policy violation / student of concern → Maxient. GBM → bit.ly/gbmreport. Minors → bit.ly/pomreport.
details: Managing the situation in front of you comes first — the report is secondary to everyone's safety. Then file the right form: Policy violation OR student of concern → Incident Report (Maxient) / bit.ly/spsconcern. All Maxient reports before 9am next day. Gender-based misconduct (harassment, assault, stalking, discrimination) → bit.ly/gbmreport Suspected abuse/maltreatment of a minor → SPS Protection of Minors report (+ 911 if danger, + NY hotline 800-342-3720, + Public Safety) Writing tip: focus on observable behaviors, give specifics (dates/counts), and avoid labels like “acting crazy.”

title: How to write an incident report (IR)
tags: incident report write writing ir maxient narrative how to fill out form objective facts third person past tense involved parties roles respondent complainant witness student of concern hall room documentation proofread lionmail
teaser: Third person, past tense, facts only (no opinions or slang). Gather date/time, location, full names+UNI, ID photos, behavior, calls. Always include Hall + Room.
steps: Before you write, gather: date/time, location, how you discovered it, full names + UNI (with roles), student ID-card photos, the behavior, who supported, calls/arrivals, and the end result.

Write: third person, past tense, facts only. Use a person's title + full name the first time, then title + last name after. Be specific enough that someone other than a Director can follow it. Proofread.

Avoid: first person, present tense, nicknames, vague umbrella terms, opinions, and slang (“weed,” “booze,” “sus,” “guilty,” “suspect,” etc.). Quote a student only if you wrote the quote down verbatim.
Facts, not labels: not “Doe was drunk” → “Doe had bloodshot eyes, an odor of alcohol, and was swaying.” Not “was rude” → quote what they actually said.

On the form: Reporter info = your full name, title, and LionMail. Under Involved Parties set each person's role (Student of Concern = wellness/FYI, no policy break; Respondent/Alleged = broke policy; Complainant/Victim; Witness; Staff Member Present) and always include Hall + Room (critical for follow-up).

Answer the prompts: “addressed with the student?” and “student aware a report is being written?” should be yes (tell them). “Immediate safety concern?” — if yes, call up. Attach supporting docs (at minimum the ID photos).

title: What happens after I file a conduct report (sanctions)
tags: conduct process sanctions hearing discipline probation gating dismissal no contact directive ncd warning certification outcome what happens after report kwesi
teaser: Report → routed to the Assistant Director → possible hearing → determination → sanctions. All violations include parental notification.
steps: After you file the Incident Report it's routed to the Assistant Director, who with a Coordinator decides whether the discipline process applies.

If it does: the student gets an email (allegation + hearing), two Pre-College staff (usually AD + 1) hold a hearing, then a determination is made — the student is presumed not responsible until then — and sanctions follow if responsible.

Sanctions can include: written warning, behavioral probation, campus gating (altered curfew / off-campus restrictions / tighter boundaries), a No Contact Directive, dismissal (depart within 48 hrs), and/or no Certificate of Participation. All violations include parental notification.

Follow-up: wellness concerns go to the wellness team; conduct concerns to an RD/Admin; FYI reports are filed. Questions about an outcome → reach out to Kwesi Alleyne. Need to debrief after an incident? Reach out — that's encouraged.

title: Sexual health resources (condoms / Plan B)
tags: condom condoms safe sex sexual health plan b plan-b contraception protection birth control std emergency contraception vending machine
teaser: RAs can't hand out condoms, but can point students to the John Jay lobby vending machine + free condoms at Health Services.
steps: RAs are not permitted to distribute condoms to students — but you can share where to get them.
A safe-sex / sexual-health / Plan-B vending machine is in the lobby of John Jay Hall.
Free condoms are available at Health Services and outside the elevator on the 8th floor of Lerner Hall.
For any health concern, students can be accompanied to Health Services (John Jay, 3rd floor).

title: Key phone numbers (on-duty + emergency)
tags: phone number contact call duty sra rd public safety emergency hotline health counselor
teaser: All the numbers you'll actually need in one place.
details: Public Safety — emergency (24/7): 212-854-5555 Public Safety — non-emergency (Morningside): 212-854-2797 Off-campus emergency: 911 → then SRA/RD Carman On-Duty SRA phone (SAVE THIS): 212-814-0027 RD on Duty: 332-465-5827 SRA on Duty (JJ + Wallach): 917-364-4454 Health Services (John Jay 3rd fl): 212-854-2284 Student Health Center: 212-854-7426 NY State Child Abuse & Maltreatment Hotline: 800-342-3720 IBC office email: ibc@columbia.edu Counselor-on-duty numbers are for the Director on Duty only and are not listed here per policy. Full numbers/UNIs live in the Master Contact Sheet.

title: Who to contact for what (coordinators & admins)
tags: coordinator wellness housing finance conduct programming admin dean director email who escalation chain maintenance facilities which handles responsible point of contact call who do i call for who is responsible for
teaser: Match your issue to the right coordinator — plus the escalation chain.
details: Escalation chain SRA → Resident Director (RD) → Assistant Director (Jeannette Sanchez) → Director. Resolve at each level before escalating. title: Training recordings & slide decks
tags: training recording recordings slides slide deck missed session zoom review onboarding learn refresher canvas catch up
teaser: Missed a training or need a refresher? Recordings + decks live in the Canvas course (Columbia login).
steps: All RA training recordings and slide decks are in the IBC Canvas course, under the Training module.
Open Canvas (Columbia login) → IBC Summer 2026 course → Training.
Recordings cover the 6/8–6/24 sessions; decks cover Res Life, Policy & Conduct, Emergency Response, International Students, Mediation, Boundaries, and more.
note: Recordings are kept inside Canvas (Columbia login required) rather than linked here, since this page is public.

title: Canvas — full RA resource library
tags: canvas courseworks course resources documents files where find everything library modules handbook policy forms slides recordings
teaser: Everything official lives in the IBC Canvas course — handbooks, policies, forms, protocols, recordings (Columbia login).
details: The IBC Summer 2026 Canvas course is the master library (Columbia login). Modules include: University & Program Policy — RA Handbook, Student Handbook, Policy Guide, Behavioral Standards Employment Resources — job description, timesheets, shift instructions, contact lists, RA teams Programming & Community Building — proposal forms, parameters, budget, excursions, samples Training — session recordings + slide decks Health & Safety — emergency/hospital protocols, Protection of Minors, Public Safety resources Reporting Forms — Wellness & Conduct, Protection of Minors, Late Curfew, CLERY
